items.title: Home & toolbar
items.description: Home button returns to the start page; toolbar grouped next to the app name (open, save, save as, PDF setup, PowerPoint export, theme toggle).

items.title: Light mode default
items.description: App opens in light mode with a smooth cross-fade theme transition; chart backgrounds update in sync with the UI.

items.title: Chart polish
items.description: Light-mode axis lines use soft slate gray instead of harsh black borders.

items.title: Choose File prompt
items.description: Data file picker button pulses orange until an Excel file is loaded.

items.title: Steel adjustment sync
items.description: Standard Analysis and Critical Six-Pack share the same per-dimension steel offset; changing one updates the other.

items.title: SPC panels
items.description: Run verdict names best/worst run by stability; Cavity Stability explains usage %; Tooling / Centering uses a distinct purple accent aligned with other cards.

items.title: Changelog modal
items.description: What's New opens a full release-notes panel organized by category.

items.title: Normality tests
items.description: Anderson–Darling (default), Ryan–Joiner, and Kolmogorov–Smirnov available on the Six-Pack tab. Side-by-side comparison shows pass/fail at α = 0.05 and highlights when a non-AD test passes but AD fails.

items.title: Capability methods
items.description: When data fail normality, choose Parametric (with warning), Percentile (non-parametric Pp/Ppk), Box-Cox, Johnson, or Taylor high-capability waiver (STAT-18). Methods are gated when not applicable (e.g., mixed cavities).

items.title: Transformations
items.description: Box-Cox and Johnson fits run automatically for non-normal data; transformed Anderson–Darling P-values (≥ 0.10 Minitab IDI rule) determine whether a transform is viable. Capability stats and histogram update for the selected method.

items.title: Normality review panel
items.description: Consolidated AD vs selected-test readout with recommended capability path and short triage note on the stats card.

items.title: SPC stability metrics
items.description: Run-level usage averages, cavity usage = |value − nominal| ÷ (tolerance ÷ 2) × 100, and risk drivers (Process Variation, Tooling Centering, Instability, Marginal).

items.title: Path Forward card
items.description: After Six-Pack analysis, a Minitab-style guide recommends the exact menu path (Normal vs Nonnormal Capability Analysis).

items.title: Transformation guidance
items.description: Suggests None, Box-Cox, or Johnson based on transformed AD results and explains why raw data failed.

items.title: Distribution context
items.description: Notes Johnson system family (Sb / Sl / Su) or post–Box-Cox evaluation steps so you can replicate the study in Minitab IDI.

items.title: One-click apply
items.description: “Apply recommended Box-Cox” shortcut when Box-Cox is the top viable transform.

items.title: Cavity Triage panel
items.description: Side-by-side mini box plots to compare cavities or runs without leaving the Critical Six-Pack tab.

items.title: Grouping modes
items.description: View by cavity (all runs), by run (all cavities), or cavity × run matrix with synced Y-axis for fair comparison.

items.title: Focus from triage
items.description: Click Focus on any box plot to filter the full six-pack to that cavity, run, or combination.

items.title: Fullscreen triage
items.description: Expand the triage grid for shop-floor review; Esc to exit.

items.title: Six-Pack expert review
items.description: Expanded condition guide for bimodal data, drift, zero-bounded GD&T, gauge resolution, and outliers with immediate and long-term actions.

items.title: SPC expert review
items.description: Dedicated common-cause vs special-cause narrative plus dashboard reading guide on the SPC tab.

items.title: Chart-level help
items.description: Pulsing insight bulbs and chart help modals for Xbar/R, capability, normality, and SPC sections with context-specific copy.

items.title: Capability & normality tooltips
items.description: Method blocks include short descriptions, applicability warnings, and links to deeper reference text.

items.title: Excel drawing export
items.description: Finds the metrology header row, preserves hidden drawing data for reload, and adds visible thumbnail previews in the workbook.

items.title: Project save/load
items.description: JSON project files retain adjustments, excluded outliers, dimension images, and settings.

items.title: Modular build
items.description: Source split into ES modules; npm run bundle produces the single-file Metrology Data Analyzer.html.

items.title: Release workflow
items.description: Version and changelog live in release/*.json; npm run release bumps the version and npm run bundle refreshes the start page.

items.title: Series filter fix
items.description: Standard analysis “Select All” can truly deselect every series; steel adjustment reloads per dimension.

releases.summary: Initial release: Standard, Six-Pack, SPC, and Action Summary workflows; PDF dimension wizard; project save/load; PPTX export; light and dark themes.
